column2: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed on Day 22. Brent traded as high as $119/bbl this week before settling near $107. Vietnam enacted an emergency 20% fuel price increase on March 19; RON95 now exceeds 30,000 VND/L. The VN-Index fell below 1,700 before rebounding to 1,813. The Govt has deployed 4-5k VND/L from the Price Stabilization Fund but reserves are running low (~2,600 bn VND remaining). PM Chinh has secured 4 million barrels from partner countries.
US launches airstrikes on Iranian nuclear facilities; Iran threatens Strait closure
IRGC Navy deploys mines and fast boats across Hormuz shipping lanes
Ship transits drop to zero; Brent surges past $86/bbl
All major carriers announce Hormuz transit suspension
Vietnam MOIT emergency fuel price adjustment; RON95 to 22340 VND/L. P&I clubs withdraw war-risk coverage.
Vietnam Govt issues Resolution 36 on petroleum price stabilization
IEA coordinates emergency SPR release. Vietnam RON95 jumps to 27047 VND/L
EIA confirms Brent at $94/bbl; Israel strikes Iran South Pars gas field
PM Pham Minh Chinh directs fuel price stabilization; Price Stabilization Fund activated
RON95 hits 29120 VND/L; first use of Price Stabilization Fund in 3 years
Govt uses heavy PSF subsidy (4-5k VND/L) holds RON95 at 25575
SBV intervenes; VN-Index falls below 1700. Free market USD/VND hits 27080
RON95 surges 20% to 30690 VND/L; diesel +24% to 33420; PM calls partners for oil supply
Brent hits $113.40. Vietnam secures 4M barrels from partners.
Day 22 of crisis. Goldman warns oil may stay triple-digits for years.
